book_claims.claim: No-sugar-added jam used as a breakfast spread in the Longevity Diet.

supplementary_research.source: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry, 2010 (PMID 20481471)
supplementary_research.finding: Blueberry anthocyanins retain 60-80% of antioxidant activity after jam processing; no-added-sugar preparations preserve polyphenol content significantly better.
Regular blueberry consumption improves endothelial function and reduces oxidative stress markers; effects maintained in processed forms when sugar content is low.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Partially absorbed in small intestine; remainder metabolized by gut microbiota into bioactive phenolic acids
Soluble fiber that gels at low pH; prebiotic effect in colon, feeds Bifidobacterium
Heat-labile; reduced in jam processing but residual amounts still present

synergies.description: Anthocyanins feed yogurt's probiotic bacteria; lactic acid from yogurt enhances anthocyanin stability
Ellagitannins in walnuts and anthocyanins act synergistically on NF-kB inflammatory pathway
Pectin in jam slows starch absorption, moderating postprandial glucose
